persistent_rooms.room_description: The Grand Pannonia Hotel lobby is a vast Belle Epoque arrival hall drenched in warm chandelier light. A sweeping staircase rises from the rear center of the room, curving upward with polished dark-wood rails and brass detailing. The floor is patterned marble with soft reflections from the ceiling lights. Reception sits toward the left side with a formal desk for guest check-in, ledger work, and concierge exchanges. On the right side of the room there is a small lounge grouping on a carpet: two burgundy upholstered chairs and a low wooden table between or just in front of them. That right-side low table is part of the established room truth and may hold small portable objects, but its position, size, material, and orientation should remain fixed unless a later room-state description explicitly changes the furniture itself. The room communicates luxury, old-world stillness, and careful order. The scene should preserve period-authentic materials including marble, polished wood, brass, velvet accents, paper stationery, and warm directional light. Suspicious details, if any, should read as subtle mystery elements within an expensive historic hotel rather than horror imagery or modern decor.
